pattern: Comparing a running left sum against a running right product at every index is a two-pass prefix/suffix sweep -- and since the product side only ever grows as more elements join it, it is safe to cap it at a sentinel above any possible sum instead of letting it explode into an unusably huge number.
hints: Precompute the running sum from the left as you go, one index at a time.
Precompute the running product from the right first, walking backwards, so it is ready before you need it at each index.

The right-side product can grow far larger than any possible left sum -- once it exceeds the maximum possible total sum of the array, clamp it to a fixed 'too big' sentinel instead of keeping the exact (huge) value, since it can only get bigger (or stay the same) as you move further left.

edge_inputs.description: balanced index in the middle of a short array
balanced index requires summing more than one left element
single element -- empty sum 0 vs empty product 1 never match
two elements, checking both empty-side conventions at once
index 0 is only balanced if the entire remaining product equals 0, which never happens since all values are >= 1
the last index is only balanced if the whole prefix sum equals the empty product 1
multiple candidate indices balance -- must report the smallest
values at the constraint ceiling so the right-hand product would explode without clamping
all ones -- sum grows slowly while product stays fixed at 1, so only an early index can balance

stress.description: random moderate values, likely no balance found, exercises the full scan and the clamp
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small values so a genuine balance is likely to occur somewhere in the array
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 3) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
